## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

When turning to the left in going from one highway to another, every vehicle shall be conducted to the right of the center of the intersection of the highway.

![chunk-0-img-6.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

...every person operating a motor vehicle or an animal-drawn vehicle on a highway shall pass to the right when meeting persons or vehicles coming toward him, and to the left when overtaking persons or vehicles going the same direction.

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-7.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-8.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-9.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-10.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Overtaking a Vehicle. – The driver of any motor vehicle overtaking another vehicle proceeding in the same direction shall pass at a safe distance to the left thereof.

![chunk-0-img-11.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-12.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-13.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-14.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Cutting an overtaken vehicle - No driver shall drive to the right side of the highway after overtaking before his motor vehicle is safely clear of such overtaken vehicle.

![chunk-0-img-15.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-16.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-17.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-18.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Driver to Give Way to Overtaking Vehicle (LANE STRADDLING) – The driver of a vehicle about to be overtaken and passed by another vehicle approaching from the rear shall give way to the overtaking vehicle on suitable and audible signal being given by the driver of the overtaking vehicle.

![chunk-0-img-19.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-20.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
FEDD

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Driver to Give Way to Overtaking Vehicle. – ... shall not increase the speed of his vehicle until completely passed by the overtaking vehicle

![chunk-0-img-21.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

roadsafety
is a SHARED responsibility.

![chunk-0-img-22.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-23.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Restrictions on Overtaking and Passing. – The driver of a vehicle shall not drive to the left side of the center line of a highway in overtaking or passing another vehicle proceeding in the same direction, unless such left side is clearly visible, and is free of oncoming traffic for a sufficient distance ahead to permit such overtaking or passing to ensure safety.

![chunk-0-img-24.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-25.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-26.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-27.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Restrictions on Overtaking and Passing. –

(b) The driver of a vehicle shall not overtake or pass another vehicle proceeding in the same direction, when approaching the crest of a grade, not upon a curve in the highway, where the driver’s view along the highway is obstructed within a distance of five hundred feet ahead, except on a highway having two or more lanes for movement of traffic in one direction where the driver of a vehicle may overtake or pass another vehicle: Provided, That on a highway within a business or residential district, having two or more lanes for movement of traffic in one direction, the driver of a vehicle may overtake or pass another vehicle on the right.

![chunk-0-img-28.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-29.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-30.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-31.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-32.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Overtaking upon a curve - No driver shall overtake or pass another vehicle proceeding in the same direction upon a curve in a highway where the driver's view along the highway is obstructed within a distance of 500 feet ahead.

![chunk-0-img-33.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-34.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)



DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
1959

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

No driver shall overtake
at any railway grade
crossing overtaking or
pass another vehicle
proceeding in the same
direction at any railway
grade crossing.

![chunk-0-img-0.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

roadsafety
is a SHARED responsibility.

![chunk-0-img-1.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-2.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RULES ON TURNING AND OVERTAKING

Overtaking at an intersection - No driver shall overtake or pass another vehicle in the same direction at any intersection of highways except on a highway having two or more lanes for movement of traffic in one direction where the driver of a vehicle may overtake another vehicle on the right.

![chunk-0-img-3.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-4.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-5.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-6.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## CHANGING OF LANES

Changing lane is not allowed if not safe.

![chunk-0-img-7.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Safe overtaking or changing of lane is allowed on the right side if the vehicle in front is visibly turning left.

![chunk-0-img-8.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Safe overtaking is allowed on the right side if there are two or more lanes going to the same direction

![chunk-0-img-9.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-10.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![Department of Transportation logo]()

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![Seal of the Department of Transportation, United States of America]()

## RIGHT-OF-WAY RULES

![chunk-0-img-11.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

- THE VEHICLE ON THE RIGHT HAS THE FIRST PRIORITY IF VEHICLES ARE GOING TO DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS
- THE VEHICLE NEAREST TO THE CURB HAS THE FIRST PRIORITY IF VEHICLES ARE GOING TO THE SAME DIRECTION
- VEHICLES INSIDE THE INTERSECTION HAS THE FIRST PRIORITY
- VEHICLES UPHILL DESERVES THE FIRST PRIORITY

![chunk-0-img-12.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![roadsafety logo]()roadsafety  
is a SHARED responsibility.

DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
FEDERAL OF AMERICA
1937

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE

## RIGHT-OF-WAY RULES

![chunk-0-img-13.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

roadsafety
is a SHARED responsibility.

![chunk-0-img-14.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-15.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RIGHT-OF-WAY RULES

Right of Way.

The driver of a vehicle upon a highway shall bring to a full stop such vehicle before traversing any “through highway” or railroad crossing: Provided, That when it is apparent that no hazard exists, the vehicle may be slowed down to five miles per hour (8kph) instead of bringing it to a full stop.

![chunk-0-img-16.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-17.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![Seal of the Department of Trade and Industry, 1850]()

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![Seal of the State of New York]()

## YELLOW BOX PROHIBITIONS

![chunk-0-img-18.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

- Do not block the intersection even when making a turn
- Do not make a U-turn
- Yield the right-of-way to those inside the yellow box
- Never communicate or exchange pleasantries with fellow motorists or road users
- Do not park within 6 meters upon intersections
- Do not load/ unload passengers or goods

Go to Main Slide

![roadsafety logo]() **roadsafety**  
is a SHARED responsibility.

DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
FEDD

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE

## RIGHT-OF-WAY RULES

![chunk-0-img-19.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

is a SHARED responsibility.

![chunk-0-img-20.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-21.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## RIGHT-OF-WAY RULES

### RIGHT OF WAY AND SPEED DE-RESTRICTION OF EMERGENCY VEHICLES

![chunk-0-img-22.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

The driver of a vehicle upon a highway shall yield the right of way to police or fire department vehicles and ambulances when such vehicles are operated on official business and the drivers thereof sound audible signal of their approach.

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-23.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![Department of Transportation logo]()

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![Seal of the State of Texas]()

## GENERAL RULES ON TURNING

![chunk-0-img-24.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![roadsafety logo]()roadsafety  
is a SHARED responsibility.

![Seal of the Department of Transportation, United States of America]()

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![Seal of the Department of Transportation, United States of America]()

## PARKING

### Parking IS PROHIBITED

a. In an intersection
b. On a crosswalk
c. Within 6 meters of the intersection
d. 4 meters from the driveway entrance
e. Within 4 meters from a fire hydrant
f. In front of a private driveway
g. On the roadway side of any unmoving or parked MV at the curb or edge of the highway
h. At any place where signs of prohibitions have been installed

![chunk-0-img-25.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-26.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-27.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-28.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-29.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-30.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

is a SHARED responsibility.

![Department of Transportation logo]()

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![Seal of the Department of Transportation, United States of America]()

## HITCHING and DRIVING AGAINST TRAFFIC

- Hitching or permitting a person or a bicycle, motorcycle, tricycle or skate roller to hitch to a motor vehicle
- Driving against traffic- failure to pass to the right when meeting persons or vehicles coming towards him

![chunk-0-img-31.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-32.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-33.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![roadsafety logo]()

![chunk-0-img-34.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-35.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## USE OF RED FLAGS

Whenever the load of any vehicle extends more than one meter beyond the bed or body thereof, there shall be displayed at every projecting end of such load a red flag not less than thirty centimeters both in length and width, except that during the hours fixed under subsection (c), there shall be displayed, in lieu of the required red flags, red lights visible at least fifty meters away.

RED FLAG LIGHTS MUST BE SWITCHED ON not later than one-half hour after sunset and until at least one-half hour before sunrise and whenever weather conditions so require, shall both be lighted.

![chunk-0-img-36.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-37.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-38.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-39.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
1925

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE

**Mufflers.** - Every motor vehicle propelled by an internal combustion engine shall be equipped with a muffler, and whenever said motor vehicle passes through a street of any city, municipality, or thickly populated district or barrio, the muffler shall not be cut out or disconnected. **No motor vehicle shall be operated in such a manner as to cause it to emit or make any unnecessary or disagreeable odor, smoke or noise.**

![chunk-0-img-40.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

roadsafety
is a SHARED responsibility.

![chunk-0-img-41.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-42.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Tires of motor vehicles. - No motor vehicle with metallic tires shall be operated upon any public highway, and solid tires whenever used shall be of sufficient thickness to prevent the metal rims thereof from coming in direct contact with the road.

Bulldozer

![chunk-0-img-43.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Backhoe

![chunk-0-img-44.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Rollers

![chunk-0-img-45.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Cranes

![chunk-0-img-46.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Go to Main Slide

**roadsafety**  
is a SHARED responsibility.

![Seal of the Department of Transportation, United States of America]()

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![Seal of the Department of Transportation, United States of America]()

## IN CASE OF A ROAD CRASH

*Duty of driver in case of accident.* - In the event that any accident should occur as a result of the operation of a motor vehicle upon a highway, the driver present, shall show his driver's license, give his true name and address and also the true name and address of the owner of the motor vehicle.

No driver of a motor vehicle concerned in a vehicular accident shall leave the scene of the accident without aiding the victim, except under any of the following circumstances:

1. If he is in imminent danger of being seriously harmed by any person or persons by reason of the accident;
2. If he reports the accident to the nearest officer of the law; or
3. If he has to summon a physician or nurse to aid the victim.

Go to Main Slide

![roadsafety logo]() roadsafety  
is a SHARED responsibility.

![chunk-0-img-47.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-48.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## OTHER DRIVING RULES of R.A. No 4136

No person shall drive or park a motor vehicle upon or along any sidewalk, path or alley not intended for vehicular traffic or parking.

![chunk-0-img-49.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-50.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

No person shall drive his motor vehicle in such a manner as to obstruct or impede the passage of any vehicle, nor, while discharging or taking on passengers or loading or unloading freight, obstruct the free passage of other vehicles on the highway.

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-51.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![chunk-0-img-52.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# ROAD AND TRAFFIC RULES

![chunk-0-img-53.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

## OTHER DRIVING RULES of R.A. No 4136

What color is the headlight of a motor vehicle?

White or yellowish white - Sec 34c

What is the allowable height of top clearance lights?

10 cms – Sec 34c

What shall be the color of taillights visible within 100 meters?

Red – Sec 34d

What shall be the color of plate light?

White – Sec 34d

What shall be the color of brake lights?

Bright Red – Sec 34e

Go to Main Slide

![chunk-0-img-54.jpeg](data:image/jpeg;base64,)
